data.body:
## Description

**What**: Create dso:completion-verifier dedicated plugin agent. Integrate into sprint at story closure (Step 10a) and Phase 7 (epic closure, between Step 0.5 and Step 1). Includes consumer smoke test for infrastructure epics.
**Why**: Independently verifies did we build what the spec says — prevents incomplete epics from being closed. Addresses the core failure where w21-24kl was closed with 62+ remaining issues.
**Scope**:
- IN: Agent definition file, sprint SKILL.md modification (story closure + Phase 7), SC/DD verification logic, consumer smoke test with discovery mechanism, remediation task creation on failure
- OUT: Test pass/fail (test gate), code quality (code review), lint/formatting (hooks)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, completion verifier is invoked at story closure in sprint Step 10a before the ticket transition command ← Satisfies SC5
- When this story is complete, completion verifier is invoked in Phase 7 between Step 0.5 (CI Verification) and Step 1 (validate-work) ← Satisfies SC5
- When this story is complete, verifier returns pass/fail per criterion with evidence ← Satisfies SC5
- When this story is complete, failures block closure and create remediation tasks ← Satisfies SC5
- When this story is complete, consumer smoke test enumerates consumers for infrastructure epics using discovery ← Satisfies SC6
- When this story is complete, consumer smoke test defines verification commands per consumer that must pass before closure ← Satisfies SC6
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Needs fixture data from closed epics to test against — gather before implementation
- [Reliability] Consumer enumeration must use discovery, not hardcoded lists — consumers change over time
- [Reliability] Remediation tasks created by the verifier must integrate with sprint existing Phase 8 remediation flow. Tasks must have correct parent epic and priority to be selected by sprint-next-batch.sh.

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
